rationale_for_baseline_tier: A rapidly expanding tonsillar/cervical mass with difficulty swallowing liquids and a partial-blockage sensation is an impending upper-airway obstruction — a time-critical, potentially life-threatening emergency that can deteriorate abruptly, compounded by high fever with rigors, systemic decompensation and the patient living alone overnight. The source clinician dispatched a priority ambulance to ED for airway monitoring. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier: sudden airway loss requires paramedic airway backup, not self-transport. The underlying suspected lymphoma is separately an urgent malignancy needing tissue diagnosis, but it is the acute airway threat that fixes the tier here.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High-pitched noisy breathing (stridor), inability to swallow own saliva, drooling, or severe difficulty pulling air in
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — tell the 000 dispatcher at once and stay on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: These signal critical upper-airway narrowing; complete obstruction can follow within minutes and needs paramedic/anaesthetic airway rescue.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing becomes noisy or high-pitched, you can't swallow your own spit, you start drooling, or it gets much harder to breathe in — tell the ambulance dispatcher immediately and keep sitting upright.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse, loss of consciousness, or turning blue
escalation_edges.rationale: Airway obstruction or systemic collapse — basic life support and immediate paramedic resuscitation are the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel you might pass out, or if breathing stops, that is an immediate emergency — 000 will guide you; if anyone is with you, have them help.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening high fever with rigors, confusion or feeling faint/clammy (systemic decompensation)
escalation_edges.rationale: May reflect sepsis or systemic inflammatory decompensation on top of the airway threat — needs the same emergency pathway and in-hospital bloods.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the fever and shaking get worse, or you feel confused, faint or clammy, tell the paramedics — this needs emergency hospital care.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no one present overnight while an airway threat is evolving
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Strengthens the case for ambulance transfer over self-transport — a sudden deterioration would be unwitnessed. Advise unlocking the door and staying on the line with 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Suspected systemic malignancy with hepatosplenomegaly and B symptoms
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure ED handover flags the suspected lymphoma so urgent ENT/haematology/oncology review and biopsy are arranged; do not let the emergency airway focus eclipse the need for urgent tissue diagnosis.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them there is a rapidly swelling throat/neck lump making it hard to swallow and breathe. Keep sitting bolt upright — do not lie down or try to sleep. Take nothing by mouth: no food and no big gulps of water. Stay as calm and still as you can and wait for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours — this is an emergency and must not wait until morning or for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will investigate the airway and the widespread swollen glands (biopsy, imaging and bloods) and provide a discharge summary; take this to your GP and the haematology/oncology team for ongoing care.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an airway-threatening rapidly expanding throat mass with swallowing difficulty as a routine or fast-track referral instead of an emergency.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Sudden overnight airway obstruction at home while living alone, with no timely paramedic airway rescue — death or hypoxic injury.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but advising self-transport to ED rather than an ambulance for an airway that can obstruct abruptly, risking airway loss en route with no clinical support.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is correct here; there is no meaningful over-triage risk given the airway threat.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute throat swelling is malignant extranodal mass, superimposed deep neck-space infection/quinsy, or both — and the true degree of airway narrowing, none resolvable via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the worst plausible cause: treat as an airway emergency, escalate to 000, and arrange urgent hospital assessment and tissue diagnosis without committing to a specific diagnosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Anchoring on a benign infective explanation and delaying escalation, OR asserting a confirmed cancer diagnosis, OR prescribing steroids/antibiotics/chemotherapy to 'cover' the uncertainty.
